name: Fascina
description: Dual linear VCA (SSI 2164) with per-channel drive, anti-click gating and an audio-rate control input. Each channel can saturate, hard-limit at 12Vpp, duck, expand or act as an envelope follower, making it a patch-programmable dynamics and distortion pair.

controls.name: Level
controls.description: Sets the initial VCA gain before any CV is applied.

controls.name: Drive
controls.description: Adds gain into the VCA so the signal hits the 12Vpp hard limiter and saturates.

controls.name: CV Attenuverter
controls.description: Attenuates or inverts the CV IN signal controlling the VCA.

controls.name: Ctrl Attenuverter
controls.description: Attenuates or inverts the audio-rate control input feeding the internal envelope follower.

controls.name: Anti-click
controls.description: Engages a zero-crossing circuit that removes clicks and zipper noise from fast gates and envelopes.

inputs.name: VCA IN
inputs.description: Main signal input of the VCA (audio or CV). One per channel.

inputs.name: CV IN
inputs.description: Voltage-control input for VCA level, routed through the CV attenuverter. One per channel.

inputs.name: CTRL IN
inputs.description: Audio-rate control input feeding the internal envelope follower for ducking, expansion, compression or sidechain-style movement. One per channel.

outputs.name: VCA OUT
outputs.description: Output of the VCA with drive, limiter and anti-click applied. One per channel.

behaviors.name: Anti-click gating
behaviors.trigger: short envelope into CV IN with Anti-click switch on
behaviors.description: The zero-crossing anti-click circuit suppresses pops on fast attacks, so percussive envelopes stay clean even at very short rise times.

behaviors.name: Audio-rate control path
behaviors.description: The control input runs through an internal envelope follower, so any audio source becomes a level-follower CV for sidechain ducking or expansion.

behaviors.name: Drive into limiter
behaviors.description: Drive pushes the signal into the 12Vpp hard limiter, turning the VCA into a soft-then-hard saturation stage with voltage-controlled distortion.

behaviors.name: Inverted ducking vs expansion
behaviors.trigger: flip Ctrl attenuverter between positive and negative
behaviors.description: Positive settings open the VCA when CTRL IN is loud (expansion), negative settings close it (ducking) - no extra inverter needed.

behaviors.name: Patch-programmable dynamics
behaviors.description: Feeding one channel's OUT into the other's CTRL IN, or sharing a CV, lets Fascina behave as a compressor, envelope follower, dual distortion or stereo VCA pair.

patchIdeas.name: Clickless pluck
patchIdeas.patch: Short envelope into CV IN · Drive low · Anti-click on · the VCA stays clean and punchy for plucks, bass notes and percussion accents.
patchIdeas.diagram:
Fascina (Ch A):
* Anti-click: on
* Drive: low
* Level: ~9 o'clock
* CV attenuverter: +70%

Envelope:
VCO:
Output:

- Envelope (Out) >> Fascina (CV IN)
- VCO (Out) -> Fascina (VCA IN)
- Fascina (VCA OUT) -> Output (In)
steps: Set Level to about 9 o'clock and Drive fully down so only envelope opens the VCA.
Flip Anti-click ON for the channel you are patching.
Patch a short pluck envelope (fast attack, short decay) into CV IN and turn the CV attenuverter roughly +70%.
Send your VCO into VCA IN and VCA OUT into your mixer or output.
patchIdeas.signalOut: A clean, click-free plucked note that starts and ends silently even with very fast envelopes.
patchIdeas.hear: Tight, punchy tone with no pop or zipper noise on the attack, like a well-behaved analog VCA.

patchIdeas.name: Sidechain ducking
patchIdeas.patch: Kick into CTRL IN · ctrl attenuverter set negative · pad into VCA IN · the internal envelope follower pulls the pad down on every kick without a dedicated compressor.
patchIdeas.diagram:
Fascina (Ch A):
* Level: ~2 o'clock (pad fully open)
* Drive: low
* Ctrl attenuverter: negative (~-70%)
* Anti-click: on

Kick:
Pad:
Mixer:

- Pad (Out) -> Fascina (VCA IN)
- Kick (Out) -> Fascina (CTRL IN)
- Fascina (VCA OUT) -> Mixer (In)
- Kick (Out) -> Mixer (In)
steps: Patch the pad into VCA IN and set Level to about 2 o'clock so the pad is fully open at rest.
Patch the kick into CTRL IN and turn the Ctrl attenuverter counter-clockwise past center (negative).
Send VCA OUT to the mixer, and send the kick itself to another channel of the mixer.
Play the kick - the pad should dip in volume on every hit; dial the Ctrl attenuverter deeper negative for more duck.
patchIdeas.signalOut: Pad audio that ducks down in volume each time the kick's envelope follower fires, then recovers.
patchIdeas.hear: A classic pumping sidechain groove - the pad breathes around the kick without any external compressor.

patchIdeas.name: Voltage-controlled saturator
patchIdeas.patch: Audio into VCA IN · Drive past noon · Level trimmed back · Fascina hits its 12Vpp hard limiter and becomes a compact saturator and brick-wall preamp.
patchIdeas.diagram:
Fascina (Ch A):
* Drive: 2-3 o'clock
* Level: trimmed to taste
* Anti-click: on

Audio Source:
Filter:
Output:

- Audio Source (Out) -> Fascina (VCA IN)
- Fascina (VCA OUT) -> Filter (In)
- Filter (Out) -> Output (In)
steps: Send any hot audio source (drums, bass, a line-level instrument) into VCA IN.
Turn Drive up past 12 o'clock until the signal visibly clips into the hard limiter.
Pull Level back so the output sits at a sensible volume coming out of VCA OUT.
Run VCA OUT into a filter to tame the new harmonics, then on to your mixer.
patchIdeas.signalOut: The same signal, now clipped against the 12Vpp limiter with added harmonic content and a flatter dynamic envelope.
patchIdeas.hear: A gritty, glued preamp tone - louder and denser, with brick-wall peaks instead of transient spikes.

patchIdeas.name: Stereo compressor
patchIdeas.patch: Stereo mix split across both channels · sum of L+R into both CTRL INs · matched settings give a simple linked stereo compressor with dual distortion on tap.
patchIdeas.diagram:
Fascina (both channels matched):
* Drive: noon
* Level: ~2 o'clock
* Ctrl attenuverter: negative (~-50%, same both channels)
* Anti-click: on both

Stereo Source L:
Stereo Source R:
Mixer (stereo):
Mult:

- Stereo Source L (Out) -> Fascina (Ch A VCA IN)
- Stereo Source R (Out) -> Fascina (Ch B VCA IN)
- Stereo Source L (Out) -> Mult (In)
- Stereo Source R (Out) -> Mult (In)
- Mult (Out) -> Fascina (Ch A CTRL IN)
- Mult (Out) -> Fascina (Ch B CTRL IN)
- Fascina (Ch A VCA OUT) -> Mixer (L)
- Fascina (Ch B VCA OUT) -> Mixer (R)
steps: Patch the left side of your stereo source into channel A VCA IN and the right side into channel B VCA IN.
Use a mult (or passive splitter) to send a mix of L+R into both channels' CTRL IN.
Set both Ctrl attenuverters to the same negative value (around -50%) so loud peaks duck both channels together.
Match Level, Drive and Anti-click across both channels; push Drive up gently for glued stereo distortion.
patchIdeas.signalOut: A stereo signal that is level-controlled by its own combined loudness, giving linked gain reduction on transients.
patchIdeas.hear: A classic 'glued' stereo bus - peaks pulled in, body thickened by drive, stereo image held together instead of collapsing.
